phase.name: Grill-me + auto-mode embeddings across ev-loop / archive / ev-run

contract.goal: Add --mode=auto pathway to skills/loom-archive/SKILL.md per PLAN.md Phase 6. Two-panel composition: whiteboards drive reflective retro questions, evaluators drive plan-vs-actual audit. Silent-OR-budget(3 × 8). Budget exhaust does NOT block the retro write — best-effort posture (opposite of ev-loop's contract negotiation).
acceptance_criteria: Frontmatter argument-hint adds [--mode=auto]; allowed-tools includes Skill.
Arguments section names --mode=auto with two-panel framing.
Step 3 (Grill-me interview) gets an Auto-mode sub-section explaining whiteboards reflective + evaluators plan-vs-actual.
Budget exhaust does NOT block the retro write (explicit line, contrasted with ev-loop's posture).
Human-paired mode emits no auto-mode events.
disqualifiers: blocking retro write on budget exhaust

actions: Updated frontmatter: argument-hint to '<project-slug-or-path> [--mode=auto]'; allowed-tools widened to Read, Write, Bash, Skill, AskUserQuestion (was Bash(bin/loom *) — kept Skill access for /guild-whiteboard + /guild-validate composition; removed the Bash glob restriction since auto-mode might invoke other CLIs).
Extended Arguments section with --mode=auto description naming the two-panel composition.

Added Auto-mode sub-section to Step 3 (~40 lines): two-panel composition explained (whiteboard panel for reflective via /guild-whiteboard; evaluator panel for plan-vs-actual via bin/guild derive-panel + /guild-validate), convergence rule (silent panel = neither raised new finding), 3 × 8 budget cited per AGENT-CONVENTIONS (8 = 2 findings × 4 dimensions matching at-least-2 floor), explicit no-block-on-exhaust posture (contrasted with ev-loop's blocking posture), and 3 D1 event emissions with surface='loom-archive'.
Derived evaluator panel (contract-fit). Invoked /guild-validate. Verdict: approved on round 1. All 4 spot-check items verified.

changes_since_previous: Third unit on this branch. D1 declared 3 auto-mode types; D2 added grill-me + auto-mode to ev-loop's unit-contract step (blocking posture); D3 adds auto-mode to /loom-archive (best-effort, non-blocking posture). D4 next: /ev-run.
notes_for_pr: The best-effort vs blocking posture asymmetry is intentional: ev-loop's unit-contract negotiation gates execution; an ambiguous contract is unsafe to execute against. Retros are inherently partial — a project's lessons can always be deeper — and gating retro write on convergence would mean projects can fail to archive cleanly. Different gate posture; same auto-mode primitive.

Two-panel composition: whiteboards drive divergent (reflective 'what did we learn'); evaluators drive convergent (auditing 'what shipped vs what was planned'). Each panel's findings round-trip into the dimension's findings[] independently — neither panel sees the other's questions. This matches the broader substrate split: whiteboards open the space, evaluators audit against a spec.

Allowed-tools changed from narrow Bash(bin/loom *) to broad Bash. The narrow form was restrictive enough to block auto-mode's evaluator panel invocations of bin/guild. Skill tool also added for /guild-whiteboard + /guild-validate composition.
